mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the sharp unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strengthening, which drives corporate revenue growth expectations and yields positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline by at least 150 bps over 60 days in backtested recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals manufacturing weakness that compresses profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances when industrial production falls below its trend.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate inflation fears, prompting central bank hawkish repricing and sharp negative equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 1% within 5 days following CPI prints above the 0.4% threshold in over half of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observed periods despite favorable employment data.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings momentum, producing consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window or GDP growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production alongside rising consumer prices indicates stagflationary supply shocks that erode real corporate profits, causing immediate negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances when industrial production declines concurrently with CPI increases.
